title: Display reclamation suggestions when logging waste time
goal: see alternative activities I could have done with that time
motivation: reframe wasted time as opportunity cost to motivate better choices
acceptance_criteria: Given a task with is_waste=true and duration_sec>0, when the task is stopped via POST /tasks/{id}/stop, then the response includes a reclamation_suggestions array
Given 45 minutes of wasted time, when suggestions are calculated, then at least 2 alternative activities are shown (e.g., '1 Spanish lesson', '3 km walk')
Given the suggestion calculation, when duration varies, then suggestions scale proportionally (e.g., 90 min = 2 Spanish lessons)
Given the API response in internal/handlers/handlers.go StopTask, when is_waste is true, then the response JSON includes reclamation_suggestions field
Given the frontend in web/static/js/app.js, when a waste task is stopped, then suggestions are displayed in the task card or a toast notification

title: Create configurable activity equivalents for reclamation suggestions
goal: define a set of alternative activities with time-per-unit values
motivation: enable accurate and varied reclamation suggestions
acceptance_criteria: Given a new activities table in internal/db/db.go schema, when migrated, then columns include id, name, minutes_per_unit, unit_label (e.g., 'lesson', 'km')
Given seed data, when the app starts, then default activities exist: Spanish lesson (30 min), Walking (15 min/km), Reading (20 min/chapter), Meditation (10 min/session)
Given an activity repo in internal/db/, when activities are queried, then all configured activities are returned
Given the reclamation calculation, when duration_sec is provided, then each activity returns count = floor(duration_sec / (minutes_per_unit * 60))

title: Create time budget model and storage
role: user who wants to limit guilty pleasures
goal: define weekly time budgets for specific categories
acceptance_criteria: Given a new budgets table in internal/db/db.go schema, when migrated, then columns include id, category_id (FK to categories), weekly_limit_sec, created_at, updated_at
Given internal/db/budgets.go, when a budget repo is created, then it supports Create, Get, Update, Delete, and ListAll operations
Given internal/models/models.go, when Budget model is added, then it includes ID, CategoryID, WeeklyLimitSec, CreatedAt fields
Given a category with a budget, when the budget is deleted, then the category remains unaffected

title: Expose budget CRUD API endpoints
goal: create, read, update, and delete time budgets via REST
motivation: allow users to manage their guilty pleasure limits
acceptance_criteria: Given internal/handlers/handlers.go, when GET /api/budgets is called, then all budgets are returned with category details
Given POST /api/budgets with {category_id, weekly_limit_sec}, when valid, then a new budget is created and returned
Given PATCH /api/budgets/{id} with {weekly_limit_sec}, when valid, then the budget is updated
Given DELETE /api/budgets/{id}, when called, then the budget is removed and 204 is returned
Given a duplicate budget for the same category_id, when POST is attempted, then 409 Conflict is returned

title: Calculate current week budget consumption
role: user tracking their guilty pleasure budget
goal: see how much of my weekly budget I have used
motivation: know when I'm approaching or exceeding my limits
acceptance_criteria: Given internal/db/budgets.go, when GetBudgetStatus(categoryID) is called, then it returns {budget, used_sec, remaining_sec, percentage_used}
Given the current ISO week, when used_sec is calculated, then it sums duration_sec from tasks where category_id matches and started_at is within the current week
Given internal/db/tasks.go List function, when filtering by week, then the from/to parameters correctly bound Monday 00:00 to Sunday 23:59
Given a category with no tasks this week, when status is queried, then used_sec is 0 and remaining_sec equals weekly_limit_sec

title: Add budget status to API response
goal: receive budget consumption data when fetching budgets
motivation: display progress bars and warnings to users
acceptance_criteria: Given GET /api/budgets, when budgets are returned, then each budget includes used_sec, remaining_sec, and percentage_used for the current week
Given GET /api/budgets/{id}, when a single budget is fetched, then it includes the same consumption fields
Given internal/models/models.go, when BudgetStatus struct is added, then it embeds Budget plus UsedSec, RemainingSec, PercentageUsed

title: Warn user when approaching budget limit
role: user logging time in a budgeted category
goal: receive a warning when I'm near or over my weekly limit
motivation: prevent accidentally exceeding my guilty pleasure budget
acceptance_criteria: Given a task is stopped via POST /tasks/{id}/stop, when the task's category has a budget, then the response includes budget_warning if percentage_used >= 80%
Given percentage_used >= 80% and < 100%, when warning is returned, then message says 'Approaching limit: X% used'
Given percentage_used >= 100%, when warning is returned, then message says 'Budget exceeded: X% used'
Given a task in a category without a budget, when stopped, then no budget_warning field is included

title: Display budget management UI
goal: create and edit weekly budgets for categories in the dashboard
motivation: easily configure my guilty pleasure limits
acceptance_criteria: Given web/static/js/app.js, when a 'Budgets' section is added to the categories view, then users can see existing budgets with progress bars
Given a category without a budget, when 'Set Budget' is clicked, then a form appears to enter weekly_limit in hours/minutes
Given an existing budget, when 'Edit' is clicked, then the form is pre-filled with current limit
Given a budget is saved, when the API returns success, then the UI updates without page reload

title: Display budget warnings in task UI
goal: see budget warnings prominently after stopping a tracked task
motivation: immediately know if I'm overusing a budgeted category
acceptance_criteria: Given web/static/js/app.js, when a task is stopped and response includes budget_warning, then a toast or alert displays the warning message
Given percentage_used >= 100%, when warning is displayed, then it uses a red/danger color scheme
Given percentage_used >= 80% and < 100%, when warning is displayed, then it uses a yellow/warning color scheme
Given no budget_warning in response, when task is stopped, then no warning UI appears

title: Display reclamation suggestions in task completion UI
role: user who just logged wasted time
goal: see what I could have done with the wasted time in the UI
motivation: feel motivated to make better choices next time
acceptance_criteria: Given web/static/js/app.js, when a waste task is stopped and response includes reclamation_suggestions, then suggestions are displayed below the task card or in a modal
Given multiple suggestions, when displayed, then each shows the activity name and achievable count (e.g., '1 Spanish lesson, 3 km walk')
Given no suggestions (duration too short), when displayed, then a message says 'Even small moments matter!'
Given a non-waste task is stopped, when response has no reclamation_suggestions, then no suggestions UI appears

title: Allow users to customize reclamation activities
goal: add my own activities and time estimates for reclamation suggestions
motivation: see suggestions relevant to my personal improvement goals
acceptance_criteria: Given GET /api/activities, when called, then all activities (default and custom) are returned
Given POST /api/activities with {name, minutes_per_unit, unit_label}, when valid, then a custom activity is created
Given DELETE /api/activities/{id}, when called on a custom activity, then it is removed
Given a default activity, when DELETE is attempted, then 403 Forbidden is returned
Given web/static/js/app.js, when an 'Activities' management section exists, then users can add/remove custom activities

title: SPIKE: Research optimal default activities and time estimates
goal: determine which default activities resonate with users
motivation: ensure reclamation suggestions feel achievable and motivating
acceptance_criteria: Research: What are the top 5-10 self-improvement activities users commonly track?
Research: What are realistic time estimates for one unit of each activity (e.g., Duolingo lesson = 15 min)?
Research: Should activities be categorized (fitness, learning, wellness)?
Deliverable: Document with recommended default activities and rationale
